topic: The boundary between data cleaning and feature engineering
key_concepts: Data cleaning is about correcting facts and making the dataset faithfully represent reality (e.g., fixing typos, standardizing date formats, deduplication).
Feature engineering is about representation: changing the shape of the data so a machine learning model can extract signal from it efficiently.
Fixing an impossible value (age=150) is data cleaning. Deciding to encode a missing value as a new signal feature or imputing it within a cross-validation loop is feature engineering.
This book focuses on the latter: decisions that alter representation and interact directly with the learning pipeline.
examples: Data cleaning: parsing string text '1,200.50' into a numeric float.
Feature engineering: applying a robust scaler to that float inside a modeling pipeline to help an SVM converge.
exclusions: Detailed tutorials on string parsing, regex for data wrangling, or database deduplication.
